name: memory-manager
tagline: ClawHub (94★): Local memory management for agents. Compression detection, auto-snapshots, and semantic search. Use when agents need to dete
verdict: Hygiene and security are solid, but the multi-step script flow (init/detect/organize/search/categorize) never says what to do when a script is missing or a path doesn't exist. Worse, categorize.sh appends without checking for existing identical entries, so re-running the same command silently duplicates records.

highlights.summary: The entire multi-step flow (init/detect/organize/search/categorize, all external script invocations) has no mention anywhere of what to do if a script is missing, a path doesn't ex
categorize.sh appends categorized content; nothing in the instructions checks for an already-existing identical entry before appending, so re-running the same categorization comman
No 'NOT for' / exclusion / out-of-scope language appears anywhere in the description or body; a general-purpose memory-management skill plausibly overlaps with other memory/knowled
